text: The thoracoscopic port, approximately 1 cm long, is located at the 7th or 8th intercostal space in the mid-axillary line, and the working port, approximately 3 to 5 cm long, at the 5th intercostal space in the operating site.
The patient is a 63-year-old man with clinical T1B and 0M0 stage squamous cell carcinoma in left low lobe.
Two instruments of operator and one instrument of assistance are usually introduced all together through the working port during operation.
At first, inferior pulmonary ligament is divided with harmonic scalpel
and then inferior pulmonary vein is dissected and a panel was situated here to guide an endostepler for division of inferior pulmonary vein later.
The pleura overlying the interlobar portion of left pulmonary artery is divided.
and then the permeatory branches to the left low lobe are dissected.
The inferior pulmonary vein is divided with an endostepler under Penrose guided approach.
The pulmonary artery branches to the left lower lobe are sequentially divided with endosteplers under Penrose guided approach.
Pan analysis is very useful to protecting the structure around the perma-atrial vein.
Four centimeter walking forward, we can use the conventional instrument.
A small pulmonary artery branch is divided with interlocking endoclips.
The anterior and posterior aspects of fissure are divided with endosteplars.

The bronchus to the left lower lobe is dissected circumferentially to the level of its takeoff, taking care to relieve associated lymph nodes with the specimen, and it is divided with an endo-stapler.
The specimen is retrieved with endo-bag through the working port.
After the left lower lobe lobectomy, mediastinal lymph node dissection is continued.
During subcarinal lymph node dissection, an assistant retracts the resected lower lobe bronchus with teri-dissector to get the sufficient space to the operator.
During aortic area lymph node dissection, an assistant retracts the upper lobe with the lymphocept to get the operative field.
During paratracheal lymph node dissection, an assistant retracts the left main pulmonary artery to get the enough space.
Two-incision VET lobectomy is applicable in selected cases.
However, when needed, conversion to the standard VETs or sarcotomy should be performed without agitation.
Thank you for your attention.
